zh.lifeSummary: 當Alicia Hankin於1559年3月3日出生在英國賀特福爾辛沃斯時，她的父親Edmund Hankyns24歲，母親Johanna Gamon19歲。她於1584年6月21日與George Inkerstall在英國英格蘭賀特福爾辛沃斯結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1644年在英國英格蘭貝德福爾艾德沃斯享年85歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Alicia Hankin于1559年3月3日出生在英国賀特福爾辛沃斯时，她的父亲Edmund Hankyns24岁，母亲Johanna Gamon19岁。她于1584年6月21日与George Inkerstall在英国英格兰賀特福爾辛沃斯结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和3个女儿的父母。她去世于1644年在英国英格兰貝德福爾艾德沃斯享年85岁。

nameMeanings.name: Hankin
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Yaron, Abbe, Isadore, Myer, Yitzhak.

English: from the Middle English personal name Hankin, a pet form of Hann , which in English usage was usually a short form of Hanry (Henry), and occasionally perhaps a rhyming pet form of Randal (Randolph). In Middle Dutch, Hankin was a pet form of Johan (John), a usage which may have been brought to England by Flemings after the Norman Conquest, but no examples have yet been found in Middle English where a man named John was also called Hankin. Compare Henkin , Hanks , and Hancock .
English: from Middle English Handekin, a pet form of the personal name Hand, Hond (see Hand ).

variants.name: Hankinson
meanings: English (Lancashire and Cheshire): from the Middle English personal name Hankin + -son.

variants.name: Hanken
meanings: English (Northumberland): variant of   Hankin  . This form of the surname is now rare in Britain.
North German: variant of   Hanke   and   Hanneken  .

variants.name: Hanks
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Hanke, with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s. In England this personal name was a shortened form of both Hankin and Hancock , both of which were used as pet forms of Henry . The equivalent Middle Dutch names Hanke and Hankin, however, had the sense ‘young John’ and may have been introduced to England by Flemish settlers after the Conquest. There is no evidence to support the suggestion that an English hereditary surname was formed from the Old Norse personal name Anki, a diminutive of personal names such as Arngrim and Arnketill.

variants.name: Hann
meanings: German and Dutch: from a shortened pet form of the personal name Johann (see John ).

English: from the Middle English personal name Hann, commonly a pet form of Henry (Middle English Hanry). Hann could also be a rhyming pet form of Randolph (Middle English Randal, shortened to Ran or Rand). Han was also a pet form of Joh(a)n in Middle Dutch, and it is possible that this usage was brought to England by Flemings after the Norman Conquest, but no evidence has yet been found that it was similarly used in Middle English. See Henry and Randall , and compare Hankin , Hancock , and Hanks .
English: variant of Ann with prosthetic H-.

variants.name: Hawken
meanings: English (Cornwall and Devon):

from Middle English Haw, a rhyming pet form of Raw (Ralph), + the diminutive suffix -kin. Hawkyn or Haukyn mean ‘young Ralph’. However, in 13th- and 14th-century handwriting -u- and -n- are often written identically, and some examples of Haukyn may therefore belong with Hankin . In The Vision of William concerning Piers the Plowman (written before 1387?) Langland named his fictional everyman figure ‘Haukyn the actyf man’, which strongly implies that in the late 14th century Haukin was a recognizable pet form of an everyday personal name, as Ralph was (see Ralph ). Compare Haw 1 and Dawkins .

sometimes perhaps a variant of Alkin with prosthetic H- and a post-1400 vocalization of Halk- to Hawk-. Alkin was a Middle English personal name, a pet form of names such as Alan, Alexander, and Alice + the diminutive suffix -kin. Compare Alcock .

variants.name: Hankins
meanings: English: variant of Hankin , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s.

nameMeanings.name: Alicia
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Modern Latinate form of   Alice .  It was borne by the British ballerina  Alicia Markova  (born  Lilian Alicia Marks ,  1910–2004 ).

variants.name: Alisha
meanings: Modern Latinate form of   Alice .  It was borne by the British ballerina  Alicia Markova  (born  Lilian Alicia Marks ,  1910–2004 ).

variants.name: Alisia
meanings: Modern Latinate form of   Alice .  It was borne by the British ballerina  Alicia Markova  (born  Lilian Alicia Marks ,  1910–2004 ).

variants.name: Alyssa
meanings: Variant spelling of   Alissa .

variants.name: Alissa
meanings: Contracted form of   Alicia .

variants.name: Alesha
meanings: Respelling of   Alicia .

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: When Alicia Hankin was born on 3 March 1559, in Hinxworth, Hertfordshire, England, her father, Edmund Hankyns, was 24 and her mother, Johanna Gamon, was 19. She married George Inkerstall on 21 June 1584, in Hinxworth, Hertfordshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 3 daughters. She died in 1644, in Edworth, Bedfordshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 85.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Alicia Hankin est née le 3 mars 1559, à Hinxworth, Hertfordshire, Angleterre, son père, Edmund Hankyns, avait 24 ans et sa mère, Johanna Gamon, avait 19 ans. Elle a épousé George Inkerstall le 21 juin 1584, à Hinxworth, Hertfordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 3 filles. Elle est décédée en 1644, à Edworth, Bedfordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 85 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Alicia Hankin am 3. März 1559 in Hinxworth, Hertfordshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Edmund Hankyns 24 und ihre Mutter Johanna Gamon 19. Sie hat am 21. Juni 1584 in Hinxworth, Hertfordshire, England, United Kingdom George Inkerstall geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Sie starb 1644 im Alter von 85 Jahren in Edworth, Bedfordshire, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Alicia Hankin è nata il il 3 marzo 1559 nel Hinxworth, Hertfordshire, England, sua padre, Edmund Hankyns, aveva 24 anni e sua madre, Johanna Gamon, aveva 19 anni. Lei sposato George Inkerstall il 21 giugno 1584, nel Hinxworth, Hertfordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 3 figlie. Morì nel 1644, nel Edworth, Bedfordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 85 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Alicia Hankinが1559年3月3日にイギリスハートフォードシュアイアヒンクスウオルスで生まれた時，父Edmund Hankynsは24歳，母Johanna Gamonは19歳であった。1584年6月21日にイギリスイギリスハートフォードシュアイアヒンクスウオルスでGeorge Inkerstallと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼女は1644年にイギリスイギリスベドフォードシュアイアエドウオルスで85歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Alicia Hankin(이)가 1559년 3월 3일 영국 허트퍼드실르 이스워스에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Edmund Hankyns(은)는 24세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Johanna Gamon(은)는 19세였다. 그녀는 1584년 6월 21일 영국 잉글랜드 허트퍼드실르 이스워스에서 George Inkerstall(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1644년 영국 잉글랜드 베드퍼드실르 에드워스에서 85세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Alicia Hankin nasceu em 3 de março de 1559, em Hinxworth, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Edmund Hankyns, tinha 24 anos e sua mãe, Johanna Gamon, tinha 19 anos. Ela casou-se com George Inkerstall em 21 de junho de 1584, em Hinxworth, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 3 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1644, em Edworth, Bedfordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 85 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Alicia Hankin родилась 3 марта 1559 г. в местности хинксворт, хиртфордшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Edmund Hankyns было 24 года, а матери, которую звали Johanna Gamon, 19. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени George Inkerstall 21 июня 1584 г. в местности хинксворт, хиртфордшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 3 дочери. Она умерла в 1644 г. в местности эдворт, бедфордшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 85 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Alicia Hankin nació el 3 de marzo de 1559, en Hinxworth, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Edmund Hankyns, tenía 24 años y su mamá, Johanna Gamon, tenía 19 años. Se casó con George Inkerstall el 21 de junio de 1584, en Hinxworth, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió en 1644, en Edworth, Bedfordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 85 años.
